quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 12
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A safety testing capsule slides down a specialized vertical guide rail, passing a sensor at an initial downward speed of $4.20\text{ m/s}$. The capsule then accelerates uniformly at $6.80\text{ m/s}^2$ over a distance of $18.5\text{ m}$. Calculate the final speed achieved by the capsule at the end of this distance.

A maritime shipping manifest lists the raw material payload of an industrial barge. Convert a recorded cargo mass value of $3.65\text{ tons}$ directly into milligrams ($\text{mg}$), given that $1\text{ ton} = 1,000\text{ kg}$.

A heavy metal crate rests on the flatbed of a delivery truck. The truck accelerates forward from rest along a straight horizontal highway, causing the crate to accelerate forward at the exact same rate without slipping. Analyze the nature of the work performed on the crate by the static friction force exerted by the truck bed during this displacement.
questions.explanation: Static friction acts forward on the crate, and the crate displaces forward. Force and displacement are in the same direction — positive work.

questions.question: A maintenance technician hoists a $14.5\text{ kg}$ replacement motor up a vertical scaffolding tower, storing exactly $3,400\text{ J}$ of gravitational potential energy in the motor relative to the ground. Determine the vertical height of the motor above ground level. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

An industrial test sled with a mass of $240\text{ kg}$ is propelled from rest along a horizontal tracking rail by a high-power magnetic launcher. The launcher maintains a constant forward force of $5,200\text{ N}$ over a distance of $6.40\text{ m}$. Use energy principles to determine the final kinetic energy achieved by the sled at the end of the launch zone.
questions.explanation: $W = Fd = 5,200 \times 6.40 = 33,280\text{ J}$. Starting from rest, $KE = W = 3.33 \times 10^4\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A $0.580\text{ kg}$ soccer ball is moving horizontally at $14.0\text{ m/s}$ due north when a player deflects it. The player's foot applies an average force of $420\text{ N}$ due south for $0.0350\text{ s}$. Calculate the final velocity magnitude and direction of the ball immediately after the kick.

An automated assembly line mechanism applies a continuous horizontal force of $260\text{ N}$ to push a series of manufactured chassis along a high-friction conveyor track. The mechanism moves the track at a steady, uniform speed of $1.85\text{ m/s}$. Calculate the average power output sustained by the conveyor drive system.

An electric winching apparatus performs $5.40 \times 10^4\text{ J}$ of useful mechanical work hoisting structural steel during an operation cycle. The system has a documented efficiency rating of $72.0\%$. Determine the total quantity of electrical energy that must be supplied to the winch motor during this cycle.

A $0.0450\text{ kg}$ ballistic slug traveling horizontally at $280\text{ m/s}$ passes completely through a stationary $4.50\text{ kg}$ block of dense polymer material. The block recoils forward at $1.80\text{ m/s}$ after the impact. Calculate the final horizontal velocity of the slug as it exits the opposite side of the block.

A field engineer uses a first-class lever tool to pry up a concrete slab. The distance from the pivot fulcrum to the slab is $0.240\text{ m}$, while the distance from the fulcrum to the handle is $1.44\text{ m}$. Calculate the ideal mechanical advantage (IMA) of this lever assembly.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on work by static friction, impulse with direction changes, and efficiency input calculations.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
